title: PULSE 2026 to convene healthcare and education stakeholders in India
summary: Conference to focus on medical education, health systems and digital health discussions
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Conference to focus on medical education, health systems and digital health discussions

PULSE 2026 will bring together policymakers, experts, healthcare leaders and academicians to discuss healthcare and medical education. The conference will focus on health systems, medical education and innovation.

The agenda includes sessions starting with “The Future of Medical Education”, covering competency-based education, use of AI in classrooms and surgical and clinical training.

The event will be attended by Devendra Fadnavis, Sunetra Pawar, Hasan Mushrif and Madhuri Misal. Participants also include Vishwajeet Kadam, Dr Ramakanta Panda, Dr Muffazal Lakdawala, Ashutosh Raghuvanshi and Sunil Nair.

Sessions will include Digital Health and AI, Clinical Innovation and Education Futures. These will cover community healthcare models, virtual hospitals, AI-based learning systems and care delivery frameworks. Discussions will also include preventive and traditional medicine, chronic disease management and patient care.

The second day will focus on health systems and workforce. Sessions will include startups, policy, governance and medical ethics. Topics such as medical data sovereignty, regulatory frameworks and scalability of healthcare models will be discussed.

The conference will conclude with the Maharashtra Declaration, focused on collaborations, investments and knowledge exchange.
